goal: Make /app/dashboard read like a relationship network health workspace that tells the user what relationship risk or opportunity to act on next, while preserving all local, side-effect-free behavior.

success_criteria.description: GET /app/dashboard first viewport leads with one current network-health priority: which relationship or coverage gap needs attention, why it matters now, the source confidence, the recommended next move, and the no-side-effect boundary before broad metrics or provenance details.

Summary metrics, distribution, network gaps, opportunities, provenance, and recent activity are grouped into a readable health-to-action workflow with relationship labels, coverage context, source confidence, and review status rather than technical inventory.

GET /app/dashboard?action=run-dashboard-review shows a usable local dashboard review preview that confirms refreshed prompts, audit findings, source confidence, and exactly what remains local; reloading the same action route remains stable.

The page and action preview explicitly show that no saved record, audit report, compliance report, message, notification, automated writing call, or outside network request occurs; raw evidence IDs are not dominant primary copy.

GET /app/dashboard?scenario=empty, pending, failure keep meaningful recovery controls; page tests assert the health-to-action workflow, action safety ledger, route recovery, and copy guardrails; npm test and npm run build pass with no feature, service, API, fixture, shared UI, shell, contact, event, follow-up, chat, agent, profile, or home route edits.

out_of_scope: Changing feature contracts, services, service factories, API envelopes, route handlers, or sample data fixtures
Changing global app shell, global CSS, shared UI primitives, contacts, events, follow-ups, chat, profile, agent, or home routes
Adding live CRM, analytics, notification, messaging, storage, search, AI, compliance, audit, or network integrations
Changing dashboard aggregate, network distribution, opportunity, or provenance audit feature behavior outside the route composition layer
